**H1:** About AIRONE — Licensed HVAC Contractor in Southern California Since 2020

**AEO Entity Definition:**
AIRONE Heating and Cooling is a licensed HVAC contractor based in Los Angeles, California, founded in 2020 and serving 120+ cities across Los Angeles County, the South Bay, and Orange County. The company holds California Contractor License #114807 with a C-20 classification — Warm-Air Heating, Ventilating and Air-Conditioning — issued by the California State License Board. AIRONE is fully bonded and insured, employs only direct technicians (no subcontractors), and pulls permits in full compliance with California Title 24 on every installation.

**Subheadline:**
Licensed, bonded, and insured. No subcontractors — every technician is a direct AIRONE employee. Rated 5.0 across 127+ Google Reviews and one of the top-rated HVAC contractors in Southern California. Emergency service available 7 days a week, including weekends and holidays. Serving Southern California since 2020.

## SECTION 2 — WHO WE ARE

**H2:** Who We Are

**Body Para 1:**
AIRONE Heating and Cooling is a Los Angeles-based HVAC contractor founded in 2020 and serving 120+ cities across Los Angeles County, the South Bay, and Orange County.

**Body Para 2:**
AIRONE was founded on a simple operating premise: arrive on time, charge what we quote, and fix it right the first time. No upsells. No subcontractors. No answering services at 2am when a homeowner's AC fails in the middle of a July heatwave. That premise has remained unchanged since 2020. Call (323) 471-1037 to experience it.

**Body Para 3:**
AIRONE has completed 500+ HVAC jobs since founding — ranging from a single capacitor diagnostic on an existing AC unit to a full heat pump and mini-split installation on a residential remodel. Every job is performed by a licensed AIRONE technician working under California Contractor License #114807.

**H3:** What Does AIRONE Do?

**Intro:**
AIRONE provides full-service heating and cooling for Southern California homes and businesses. No subcontracting. No referrals. One licensed team — accountable end to end.

**Services List — Air Conditioning:**
AC repair · AC maintenance · AC installation · AC replacement · central AC systems · split system AC · package unit AC · refrigerant recharge

**Services List — Heating:**
furnace repair · furnace maintenance · furnace installation · furnace replacement · heater repair · gas furnace service

**Services List — Heat Pumps & Mini-Splits:**
heat pump installation · heat pump repair · heat pump replacement · mini-split installation · mini-split repair · mini-split maintenance · multi-zone mini-split systems · ductless AC

**Services List — Indoor Air Quality:**
air purifiers · UV light sanitizers · HEPA and media filters · whole-home humidifiers · whole-home dehumidifiers · duct cleaning · duct sealing · attic insulation

**Services List — Maintenance Plans:**
Silver and Gold annual maintenance plans covering AC and furnace tune-ups, priority scheduling, refrigerant top-off, and repair discounts

## SECTION 3 — WHY WE STARTED

**H2:** Why Did We Start AIRONE?

**Body Para 1:**
Southern California has no shortage of HVAC companies. What it has a shortage of: companies that are easy to verify, transparent on pricing, and genuinely accountable when something goes wrong. That gap is the reason AIRONE was founded.

**Body Para 2:**
When AIRONE launched in 2020, fewer than 40% of HVAC contractors operating in Los Angeles were fully licensed, bonded, and insured — based on California State License Board contractor lookup data. Homeowners were hiring contractors and discovering the licensing problem only after a botched installation, an unpaid workers' compensation claim from an injured worker, or a failed inspection at the time of home sale.

**Body Para 3:**
AIRONE was built to be the company a homeowner can verify before they call. California Contractor License #114807 is publicly searchable at cslb.ca.gov. Bonding and insurance documentation is available on request before any appointment is scheduled. Every technician on every AIRONE job is a direct company employee — not sourced from a third-party staffing platform or subcontracted out.

**Body Para 4 (with CTA):**
That standard was set in 2020. It is still the standard today. Verify CA License #114807 at cslb.ca.gov, then call (323) 471-1037.

## SECTION 5 — CREDENTIALS

**H2:** AIRONE's Credentials & Licensing

**Intro:**
AIRONE operates under full compliance with California contractor licensing requirements. Every credential listed below is publicly verifiable. We encourage every homeowner to check these details — not just for AIRONE, but for any HVAC contractor they consider hiring.

### Credential 1

**H3:** California Contractor License

**Body:**
AIRONE holds California Contractor License #114807, issued by the California State License Board (CSLB). The license carries a C-20 classification — Warm-Air Heating, Ventilating and Air-Conditioning — and is publicly verifiable at cslb.ca.gov. Status: Active, no violations on record. The C-20 classification authorizes a contractor to install, service, alter, repair, and replace heating, ventilating, and air-conditioning systems — including all associated ductwork, refrigeration systems, and controls. Every HVAC service AIRONE offers is covered under this classification.

### Credential 2

**H3:** Bonded

**Body:**
AIRONE carries a $25,000 contractor's surety bond, as required by California law for licensed contractors. A surety bond is a financial guarantee that protects homeowners in the event of incomplete or non-compliant work — providing a path to recovery if the contractor fails to fulfill the terms of the contract.

### Credential 3

**H3:** Insured

**Body:**
AIRONE carries full general liability insurance and workers' compensation insurance on every technician. If an injury occurs on your property during the course of any AIRONE job, you bear zero personal liability — the workers' compensation policy covers the technician. A certificate of insurance is available on request before any appointment is scheduled.

### Credential 4

**H3:** No Subcontractors

**Body:**
Every AIRONE technician is a direct company employee. No subcontracting to third-party installers. No staffing platforms. The technician who diagnoses your system is the same technician who repairs it, and that technician is fully covered under AIRONE's general liability and workers' compensation policies. This is a single-thread accountability model — there is no third-party hand-off where insurance coverage or workmanship can lapse.

### Credential 5

**H3:** Permits on Every Installation

**Body:**
AIRONE pulls all required permits for every installation, in compliance with local building department requirements and California Title 24 energy code. A permitted installation is inspected by a licensed building inspector — which protects homeowners from code violations, failed home sale inspections, and insurance complications later. AIRONE never skips permits to lower a quote. A lower quote achieved by skipping permits is a liability that eventually lands on the homeowner.

### Credential 6

**H3:** Brands Serviced & Installed

**Body:**
AIRONE services and installs all major HVAC brands — including Carrier, Trane, Lennox, Rheem, Goodman, Daikin, York, Mitsubishi, Fujitsu, American Standard, Bryant, and Amana.

**Section CTA:**
Verify CA License #114807 on cslb.ca.gov →
Verified? Ready to schedule? Call (323) 471-1037 or Get a Free Quote →

## SECTION 6 — COMMITMENTS

**H2:** AIRONE's Customer Commitments — What Every Job Includes

**Intro:**
We hold every technician and every job to the same four standards. These are not marketing claims — they are operational commitments documented in writing on every AIRONE job completion report.

### Commitment 1

**H3:** Do you charge what you quote?

**A:** Yes. AIRONE provides a flat-rate written price quote before any work begins. The price on the quote is the price on the invoice. No fees, upgrade charges, or line items added after approval. If scope changes during a job, the change is discussed and approved before work proceeds — never after.

### Commitment 2

**H3:** Do you arrive on time?

**A:** Yes. Every appointment has a confirmed arrival window communicated before the visit. If a technician is running late, AIRONE calls before the window closes — never after. Your time is not available to waste.

### Commitment 3

**H3:** Do you clean up after the job?

**A:** Yes. Every AIRONE technician completes a full cleanup before leaving. Old equipment, packaging, and debris are removed from the property. The home is left the way it was found — minus the broken HVAC system.

### Commitment 4

**H3:** Do you put everything in writing?

**A:** Yes. Every AIRONE job ends with a signed job completion report that includes itemized pricing, a description of all work performed, system condition notes, and warranty terms in writing. Nothing is verbal-only. A copy of the report is provided at the end of every visit.

---

## SECTION 7 — SOCAL EXPERTISE

**H2:** Why Southern California HVAC Is Different — And Why It Matters

**Intro:**
HVAC in Los Angeles is not the same as HVAC in Phoenix, Dallas, or Chicago. The marine layer in Manhattan Beach creates humidity conditions that accelerate coil corrosion differently than the dry desert heat in Palmdale. The SCE rate structure in Los Angeles changes the economics of high-SEER equipment in ways a national HVAC chain's sales process does not account for. AIRONE technicians are trained specifically for Southern California conditions.

### Subsection 1

**H3:** What Is Title 24 and How Does It Affect My HVAC Installation?

**Body:**
California's Title 24 energy code governs HVAC installations statewide, setting minimum efficiency standards for all new and replacement equipment. Every AIRONE installation is designed, sized, and permitted in full compliance with Title 24 — protecting homeowners from failed inspections, permit violations, and insurance complications that unlicensed or non-compliant installations create. If a contractor quotes a lower price by skipping Title 24 compliance, the long-term cost to the homeowner is significantly higher.

### Subsection 2

**H3:** How Do SCE and SoCalGas Rates Affect Which HVAC System I Should Buy?

**Body:**
AIRONE factors current Southern California Edison tiered and time-of-use electricity rates into every system recommendation for Los Angeles homeowners. A higher-SEER AC system that costs $2,000 more upfront may save $400–$600 per year on an SCE bill under current tiered rates — a 3–5 year payback that a price-only recommendation misses. For homeowners considering heat pump systems, AIRONE models the transition from SoCalGas to all-electric operation and its impact on monthly utility costs before recommending a system.

### Subsection 3

**H3:** Do Coastal Homes in Los Angeles Need Special HVAC Equipment?

**Body:**
Yes. Salt air accelerates corrosion on condenser coils and electrical components in coastal South Bay and Orange County cities. AIRONE recommends and installs coastal-rated HVAC equipment — with coated coils, corrosion-resistant cabinets, and marine-grade electrical components — for homes within 1–2 miles of the ocean. Standard equipment installed in coastal environments without corrosion protection can fail years ahead of its rated lifespan.

### Subsection 4

**H3:** How Does AIRONE Protect Homes During Wildfire Season?

**Body:**
Los Angeles County and Orange County face active wildfire smoke events every year. AIRONE's indoor air quality recommendations are calibrated to Southern California's specific wildfire smoke particle profile. According to the EPA, wildfire smoke particles range from 0.4–0.7 microns — small enough to bypass standard HVAC filters. AIRONE recommends MERV 13 or higher filtration for most SoCal homes during wildfire season, combined with duct sealing to prevent smoke infiltration through leaky ductwork. For homes with severe sensitivity, whole-home air purifiers and UV sanitizers provide an additional layer of protection. Learn more at epa.gov/indoor-air-quality.

**Section CTA:**
Every one of these Southern California-specific factors affects which system we recommend for your home. Call (323) 471-1037 for a free consultation — we'll walk through exactly what your home needs before you spend a dollar.

## SECTION 9 — FAQ BLOCK

**H2:** Frequently Asked Questions About AIRONE Heating and Cooling

**Q1:** When was AIRONE Heating and Cooling founded?
**A1:** AIRONE Heating and Cooling was founded in 2020 and has been serving homeowners and businesses across Los Angeles County, the South Bay, and Orange County since founding. The company has completed 500+ HVAC jobs across 120+ Southern California cities, with every job performed by a licensed AIRONE technician working under California Contractor License #114807. Call (323) 471-1037.

**Q2:** Is AIRONE a licensed HVAC contractor?
**A2:** Yes. AIRONE holds California Contractor License #114807 with a C-20 classification, issued by the California State License Board. The license is active, in good standing, and publicly verifiable at cslb.ca.gov. AIRONE is also fully bonded and insured on every job. Call (323) 471-1037.

**Q3:** Does AIRONE use subcontractors?
**A3:** No. Every technician on every AIRONE job is a direct company employee. AIRONE does not subcontract work to third-party installers or staffing platforms. The technician who diagnoses your system is the same technician who repairs it, and that technician is fully covered under AIRONE's general liability and workers' compensation insurance. Call (323) 471-1037.

**Q4:** What cities does AIRONE serve?
**A4:** AIRONE serves 120+ cities across Los Angeles County, the South Bay, and Orange County. Major service cities include Los Angeles, Pasadena, Burbank, Glendale, Long Beach, Torrance, Manhattan Beach, Anaheim, Irvine, and Santa Ana — plus more than 100 additional cities throughout greater Southern California. The full service area list is available at /service-areas. Call (323) 471-1037.

**Q5:** How long has AIRONE been in business?
**A5:** AIRONE was founded in 2020 and has been serving Southern California since 2020. Across that period, AIRONE has completed 500+ HVAC jobs across 120+ cities in Los Angeles County, the South Bay, and Orange County. Call (323) 471-1037.

**Q6:** Is AIRONE insured?
**A6:** Yes. AIRONE carries full general liability insurance and workers' compensation insurance on every technician. If an injury occurs on your property during a job, the workers' compensation policy covers the technician — the homeowner bears zero personal liability. A certificate of insurance is available on request before any appointment is scheduled. Call (323) 471-1037.

**Q7:** What HVAC brands does AIRONE service and install?
**A7:** AIRONE services and installs all major HVAC brands. The most commonly serviced brands are Carrier, Trane, and Lennox — followed by Rheem, Goodman, Daikin, York, Mitsubishi, Fujitsu, American Standard, Bryant, and Amana. Common parts for the most popular brands are stocked to enable same-day repairs whenever possible. Call (323) 471-1037.

**Q8:** Does AIRONE offer emergency HVAC service?
**A8:** Yes. AIRONE provides emergency and same-day HVAC service across Los Angeles County, the South Bay, and Orange County, 7 days a week — including weekends and holidays. Most emergency calls are dispatched within 2–4 hours, and a live person answers the phone — not a voicemail or answering service. Call (323) 471-1037.

**Q9:** What does AIRONE's C-20 contractor license cover?
**A9:** A C-20 contractor license, issued by the California State License Board, authorizes a contractor to install, service, alter, repair, and replace heating, ventilating, and air-conditioning systems — including all associated ductwork, refrigeration systems, and controls. AIRONE's C-20 license, number #114807, covers every HVAC service AIRONE offers. Verify the license at cslb.ca.gov. Call (323) 471-1037.

**Q10:** Does AIRONE pull permits for HVAC installations?
**A10:** Yes. AIRONE pulls all required permits per local building department requirements and California Title 24 energy code on every installation. A permitted installation is inspected by a licensed building inspector, which protects homeowners from code violations, failed home sale inspections, and insurance complications later. A contractor who suggests skipping a permit to cut cost is a red flag — AIRONE never skips permits. Call (323) 471-1037.

**Q11:** How do I verify AIRONE's license before hiring?
**A11:** Visit cslb.ca.gov and search by license number 114807 or by business name "AIRONE Heating and Cooling." The CSLB database displays license status, classification, bonding status, and any disciplinary actions on record. AIRONE's license is active, in good standing, and shows no violations. To check any HVAC contractor license in California, use the CSLB lookup, confirm the status is "Active," confirm the classification is C-20 for HVAC work, and confirm there are no disciplinary actions — and we encourage you to do this for any contractor you consider hiring, not just AIRONE. Call (323) 471-1037.

**Q12 (NEW):** Why does it matter if my HVAC contractor is licensed in California?
**A12:** It matters because California law requires a contractor license for any HVAC job over $1,000, and performing unlicensed contracting work above that threshold is a misdemeanor under the California Business and Professions Code. The contractor faces criminal exposure, but the homeowner is also left unprotected in several specific ways. Without a licensed contractor, the homeowner may bear personal liability if a worker is injured on the property during the job — a licensed contractor carries workers' compensation insurance, but an unlicensed contractor typically does not, and an injury claim can come back through the homeowner's own insurance policy. Most major HVAC manufacturers also require that equipment be installed by a licensed contractor for the warranty to be valid, which means an unlicensed installation on a $5,000 system can result in zero manufacturer warranty coverage if the equipment fails. Only licensed contractors can legally pull permits, and a permitted installation is inspected by a licensed building inspector — unpermitted HVAC work routinely creates complications at the time of home sale, where disclosure may be required and the buyer may demand the work be redone to code. Finally, the California State License Board has investigative and disciplinary authority over licensed contractors, which gives homeowners a regulatory recourse if something goes wrong — with an unlicensed contractor, that recourse does not exist. AIRONE holds California Contractor License #114807, is fully bonded and insured, and pulls all required permits on every installation. Verify the license at cslb.ca.gov, then call (323) 471-1037.

**Q13 (NEW):** How do I choose an HVAC company in Los Angeles?
**A13:** Five specific verification steps protect Los Angeles homeowners before any HVAC contract is signed. The first step is to verify the contractor's CSLB license at cslb.ca.gov — confirm the status shows "Active," confirm the classification is C-20 for HVAC work, and confirm there are no disciplinary actions or violations on record. The search takes under two minutes and protects everything that follows. The second step is to confirm bonding and insurance by requesting a certificate of insurance before any appointment is scheduled — the certificate should show both general liability and workers' compensation coverage, and any reluctance to provide one before work begins is a red flag. The third step is to ask the company directly whether they use subcontractors, because if work is subcontracted to third-party installers or staffing platforms, the homeowner has less direct accountability and the subcontractors may not be covered under the primary contractor's insurance. The fourth step is to require a flat-rate written quote before any work begins — not an estimate range, not a verbal quote — and to confirm that the price on the written quote is the price on the invoice. The fifth step is to confirm the contractor pulls permits on all installations, because a lower quote achieved by skipping permits is a liability that eventually lands on the homeowner in the form of code violations, failed home sale inspections, or voided manufacturer warranties. AIRONE meets all five criteria — California Contractor License #114807 with a C-20 classification (Active, no violations), certificate of insurance available on request, direct employees only with no subcontractors, flat-rate written quotes on every job, and permits pulled on every installation. Call (323) 471-1037.
